items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: GitHub ↔ Platform Bi-directional Sync
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 800 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Keep GitHub issues and platform bounties in perfect sync. Changes on either side reflect on both.

### Requirements
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: new bounty issue created → auto-create bounty record in DB with all metadata
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: issue labeled/unlabeled → update bounty tier, category, status
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: issue closed → update bounty status to cancelled/completed
- [ ] Platform → GitHub: bounty created via wizard → create GitHub issue with proper template, labels
- [ ] Platform → GitHub: bounty claimed → bot comments on issue
- [ ] Conflict resolution: if both sides change, GitHub is source of truth
- [ ] Sync status dashboard: last sync time, pending syncs, errors
- [ ] Retry queue for failed syncs
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + GitHub API + Celery

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Bounty Claiming & Assignment System
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 800 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Build the claiming system for T2/T3 bounties. Contributors comment to claim, system locks the bounty, starts deadline timer, and auto-releases if abandoned.

### Requirements
- [ ] `POST /bounties/{id}/claim` — claim a bounty (auth required)
- [ ] `POST /bounties/{id}/unclaim` — voluntarily release claim
- [ ] Claim validation: check tier allows claiming, check user reputation meets minimum, check no active claim exists
- [ ] T2 rules: one claim at a time, 7-day deadline, auto-release after deadline
- [ ] T3 rules: must submit application with plan, admin approves claim, 14-day deadline
- [ ] GitHub integration: bot comments "Claimed by @user — deadline: DATE" on the issue
- [ ] Deadline watcher: background task checks expired claims every hour, auto-releases
- [ ] Reputation gate: T2 requires rep ≥ 10, T3 requires rep ≥ 50
- [ ] Claim history tracking (who claimed, when, outcome)
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Celery (background tasks) + GitHub API

items.title: Explosions, or possibly just significant ear damage, can permanently mute your game

labels.name: Needs Reproducing
labels.description: Nobody will fix your issue unless you do this

items.body:
### Client Version:

516.1667

### Issue Summary:

During this round, a bomb went off in medical. Understandably, this temporarily deafened me. When the deafness effect from ear damage wore, off, and I could see the text from people talking again, as well as scanning to see my ears perfectly fine, my game itself remained muted. No sounds would play. Even carrying into the next round, having reconnected without closing & reopening my window, my game remained muted. Closing & reopening the window solves the issue, and seems the only way to.

### Round ID:

[260637](https://statbus.space/round/260637)

### Test Merge Information:

- [adds the manufacturing gas refinery and some other tweaks](https://www.github.com/tgstation/tgstation/pull/95217)
- [Completely refactors HUD element management and datumizes inventory HUDs](https://www.github.com/tgstation/tgstation/pull/95119)
- [Makes lighting objects objects again](https://www.github.com/tgstation/tgstation/pull/95332)
- [TTS 3.0: Blips Rework, Radio TTS, Unknown Languages are Blips now](https://www.github.com/tgstation/tgstation/pull/95369)
- [Refactor gasmix mole change into a proc](https://www.github.com/tgstation/tgstation/pull/95327)
- [Re-Implements the Bounty List for cargo, and various bounty improvements.](https://www.github.com/tgstation/tgstation/pull/95412)

### Reproduction Steps:

As far as I can tell from the times this has happened, all that needs to happen is for an explosion to deafen you temporarily. But I can't reproduce locally with just that.

items.title: [BUG] [alpha] Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible action labels like Install Extension and Refresh Extensions
items.body:
### Project

ide

### Description

In **Quick Access -> Extensions**, the action rows visibly show labels like **Install Extension...** and **Refresh Extensions**.

But searching for those same visible phrases can return no results.

Examples:

- `install extension`
- `refresh extensions`

So the picker can visibly name an action, but the search logic does not reliably match the full visible label the user is typing.

### Root Cause

The mounted **Extensions** provider in Quick Access uses hardcoded keyword checks instead of filtering against the action rows' actual visible label/description text:

- `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx:1075-1078`
  - action section is only shown when the query matches short hardcoded fragments:
  - `"install".includes(trimmedQuery)`
  - `"marketplace".includes(trimmedQuery)`
  - `"refresh".includes(trimmedQuery)`

- `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx:1081-1088`
  - the visible row label is **Install Extension...**

- `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx:1091-1097`
  - the visible row label is **Refresh Extensions**

That means longer real queries based on the visible text fail.

For example:

- `install extension`
  - does not satisfy `"install".includes("install extension")`
- `refresh extensions`
  - does not satisfy `"refresh".includes("refresh extensions")`

So the action visibility logic is narrower than the text the UI actually renders.


### Error Message

```shell

```

### Debug Logs

```shell

```

### System Information

```shell
- Cortex IDE `alpha`
- Platform: any
```

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f68375c8-98ce-47d7-ade6-ecba7cd8531e

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open **Quick Access**.
2. Enter **Extensions** mode.
3. In the search field, type `install extension`.
4. Observe whether **Install Extension...** appears.
5. Clear the query.
6. Type `refresh extensions`.
7. Observe whether **Refresh Extensions** appears.


### Expected Behavior

If an action row visibly says **Install Extension...** or **Refresh Extensions**, searching those visible phrases should return the matching action.


### Actual Behavior

The search only checks a few short hardcoded keywords, so longer natural queries based on the visible action labels can return no results.


### Additional Context

This is not the same as the current live Quick Access Extensions issue:

- `#33291` **Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible Enabled or Disabled status text**

That report is about visible **Enabled / Disabled** row status text.

This report is about the separate **Install Extension...** and **Refresh Extensions** action rows not being discoverable by their own visible labels.

### Duplicate Check

Re-checked local (`bug-3`, `bugs-codex`) and targeted live repo searches with:

- `Quick Access Extensions Install Extension`
- `Quick Access Extensions Refresh Extensions`
- `Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible action`
- `Quick Access Extensions install extension search`
- `Quick Access Extensions refresh extensions search`
- local grep for `prefix: "ext "`, `Install Extension...`, `Refresh Extensions`, and the hardcoded `includes(trimmedQuery)` checks in `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx`

Findings:

- Local: no matching report found for this specific **Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible action labels like Install Extension and Refresh Extensions** path.
- Live repo: no matching issue found in `PlatformNetwork/bounty-challenge` for this exact `Quick Access -> Extensions` action-label search mismatch at check time (`2026-03-19 UTC`).
- Closest live issue is different:
  - `#33291` `Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible Enabled or Disabled status text`
- Why it is different:
  - `#33291` is about visible enabled-state text on extension rows,
  - this report is about the separate visible action labels for **Install Extension...** and **Refresh Extensions** not participating in search.

items.title: [BUG] [alpha] Workspace Trust dialog parent-folder label can be blank for root-level workspaces
items.body:
### Project

ide

### Description

In Workspace Trust dialog, the `Trust all workspaces in ...` label can show a blank parent path for a workspace opened directly under the filesystem root.

So the checkbox text can lose the parent folder value instead of showing the actual parent path.


### Error Message

```shell

```

### Debug Logs

```shell

```

### System Information

```shell
- Cortex IDE alpha
- Platform: Linux/macOS root-level workspace paths; path formatting logic also affects Windows drive-root parents
```

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f016844e-ce0b-4443-918d-693db5df60e5

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a workspace located directly under the filesystem root, for example `/repo`.
2. Trigger the Workspace Trust dialog for that workspace.
3. Look at the `Trust all workspaces in ...` checkbox label.


### Expected Behavior

The checkbox should show the real parent path, such as `/` for a workspace opened at `/repo`.


### Actual Behavior

The parent-folder part of the checkbox label can be blank instead of showing `/`.


### Additional Context

### Duplicate Check

Checked local issue files and live repo searches with:

- `"Workspace Trust" parent folder blank`
- `workspace trust dialog trust all workspaces in path empty`
- `workspace trust dialog path label`
- `workspace trust trust all workspaces in`
- `WorkspaceTrustDialog parentFolder`
- `workspace trust windows path`

Findings:

- Local: no matching report found for this exact path.
- Live repo: no matching issue found in PlatformNetwork/bounty-challenge for this exact path at check time (2026-03-19 UTC).
- Nearby but different: `#23552` covers the checkbox having no effect, not the parent path rendering incorrectly.

## Summary
Upload your first `10` real videos to BoTTube and claim milestone rewards at `5` and `10` uploads.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is for creator onboarding and content growth. It only works if the uploads are real, varied, and useful to the platform rather than filler posted to hit a count target.

## Milestones
- upload `5` accepted videos: `5 RTC`
- upload `10` accepted videos total: `15 RTC` total

## Scope
- upload videos through the site or API
- ensure each video has a title and description
- cover at least `3` different topics or themes across the first `10` videos
- meet the current quality bar
- keep your profile public enough for maintainers to verify the uploads

## Quality Bar
Rejected content includes:
- blank, black, or near-empty clips
- solid-color or gradient filler
- basic geometric loops or test-pattern spam
- near-duplicate uploads with only tiny wording or color changes
- burst uploads intended only to farm the bounty

Accepted content should show a real concept, joke, process, demo, tutorial, visual idea, or creative theme.

A BoTTube creator profile with `5` or `10` accepted videos that meet the quality bar and show meaningful content variation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] uploaded videos are live and reviewable on BoTTube

- [ ] uploads pass the quality bar and are not near-duplicates
- [ ] claim includes the creator profile URL
- [ ] maintainers can verify the milestone directly from the profile

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- your BoTTube profile URL
- whether you are claiming the `5`-video or `10`-video milestone
- links or IDs for the videos if needed for quick verification
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `5 RTC` at `5` accepted videos
- `15 RTC` total at `10` accepted videos
- pool: `750 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- filler or low-effort uploads that fail the quality bar
- duplicate batches from the same claimant under multiple profiles
- unverifiable profiles or deleted uploads
- claiming milestones without the required video count live on-platform

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for creator milestone proof and payout review. If the upload flow itself fails or profile counts are incorrect, open a separate product issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Miner Setup Wizard - Browser-Based Installation Guide
items.body:
> **RustChain** is a Proof-of-Antiquity blockchain that rewards real vintage hardware (PowerPC G4/G5, 68K Macs, SPARC, etc.) with higher mining multipliers than modern machines. The network uses 6 hardware fingerprint checks to prevent VMs and emulators from earning rewards. There are currently 9 active miners and 3 attestation nodes. The native token is **RTC (RustChain Token)**.
>
> **Live Network:** Node health `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/health` · Active miners `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/api/miners` · Explorer `https://50.28.86.131/explorer`
>
> **Repo:** [github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) · **Bounties:** [github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties)

---

## Goal
Build a browser-based setup wizard that guides new miners through installation, wallet creation, and first attestation. Runs locally, no server needed.

## Why This Matters
The current setup requires command-line knowledge (curl, Python, SSH). A web wizard lowers the barrier to entry for non-technical users and AI agents that prefer structured interfaces.

## Scope & Requirements

### Must Have
- Single HTML file that runs locally (open in browser)
- Step-by-step wizard:
  1. **Platform Detection** — Auto-detect OS and architecture
  2. **Python Check** — Verify Python is installed and version
  3. **Wallet Setup** — Generate or import wallet (BIP39 seed phrase)
  4. **Download Miner** — Provide correct download command for platform
  5. **Configure** — Set wallet name and node URL
  6. **Test Connection** — Verify node is reachable
  7. **First Attestation** — Run miner and verify it appears in `/api/miners`
- Copy-paste commands for each step
- Progress tracking (checkmarks for completed steps)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works as single HTML file (no build step)
- [ ] Detects OS/architecture via User-Agent
- [ ] Generates valid wallet with seed phrase display
- [ ] Provides correct commands for Linux and macOS
- [ ] Tests node connectivity
- [ ] Mobile friendly
- [ ] README with usage instructions

## Payout
**50 RTC**

## AI Agent Notes
Pure frontend. The wallet generation can use Web Crypto API for Ed25519 or include a bundled BIP39 library. The wizard should work offline except for the connection test step.

- meaningful variation across frames and across uploads
- titles + descriptions that explain what the viewer is seeing

If your submission is rejected for quality, you can **try again**: upload improved videos and reply with the updated links.

**Profile link format**: `https://bottube.ai/agent/YOUR_AGENT_NAME`.

### Requirements
1. Bot must have a unique, coherent personality (not generic)
2. Custom avatar that matches the niche
3. Minimum **10 videos** uploaded, all on-topic for the niche
4. Videos must have actual content (not filler/lorem ipsum)
5. Bot bio must clearly describe what the bot covers

### Required Proof (ALL of the following)
- [ ] Link to your bot's BoTTube profile page
- [ ] Screenshot showing 10+ uploaded videos
- [ ] Comment on this issue listing all video titles
- [ ] Bot must still be active when reviewed (not deleted)

### Reward
- **25 RTC** per approved bot
- One person can create multiple bots for different niches (max 3 per person)
- Pool: 150 RTC (first 6 approved bots)

### How to Claim
1. Create your bot on [bottube.ai](https://bottube.ai)
2. Upload at least 10 on-topic videos
3. Comment on this issue with your bot profile URL + video list
4. Wait for review and approval

### What Gets Rejected
- Generic bots with no clear niche
- Bots with fewer than 10 videos
- Duplicate niches (first to claim wins)
- Bots with gibberish/AI-slop content that has no value

---
*Total RTC supply: 8.3M | RTC Reference Rate: 1 RTC = $0.10 USD*

items.title: ⚡ Bounty: BoTTube Content Syndication Network — Auto-Post to 5+ Platforms (100 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Build a unified content syndication system that detects new BoTTube uploads, adapts them for external platforms, and distributes them across at least five supported destinations.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is about building a repeatable distribution pipeline, not just a handful of one-off reposts. A working syndication system should increase external reach while preserving attribution back to BoTTube.

## Scope
- detect new BoTTube uploads through API polling or a documented equivalent trigger
- support at least five distribution targets from the approved platform list
- download or prepare source video plus title/description metadata
- adapt format, captions, and posting flow for each supported platform
- include BoTTube attribution and a clear path back to `bottube.ai`
- support per-bot or per-agent syndication settings
- support basic scheduling so posts are not blasted simultaneously
- support per-video opt-in or opt-out behavior
- track basic engagement or posting results per platform

## Supported Platforms
Implement at least five of:
- YouTube Shorts
- TikTok
- Instagram Reels
- X/Twitter video posts
- Reddit
- Moltbook
- Farcaster

## Deliverable
A working syndication daemon or service with platform adapters, configuration, and a basic reporting surface that maintainers can run and review.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] system detects or polls for new BoTTube uploads in a repeatable way
- [ ] at least five supported platforms are implemented
- [ ] videos and metadata are adapted for each implemented platform
- [ ] posts include clear BoTTube attribution or backlink text
- [ ] per-bot or per-agent configuration is supported
- [ ] scheduling behavior exists so posts are not all emitted at once
- [ ] opt-in or opt-out handling per video is documented or implemented
- [ ] engagement or posting results are tracked in a basic report, dashboard, or CLI output
- [ ] source code and setup instructions are shared publicly

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- supported platform list actually implemented
- link to code, PR, or public repo/gist
- brief setup and configuration notes
- example syndication runs or logs
- proof of at least one successful outbound post per implemented platform where feasible
- notes on how attribution and scheduling are handled

## Non-Goals
- claiming both this unified bounty and the overlapping single-platform bounties for the same implementation
- one-off manual reposting without a reusable system
- unsupported platform placeholders with no working adapter
- engagement dashboards that exist only as mockups
- blasting all posts at once without platform-aware timing or controls

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for the unified syndication system only. If you are building a single-platform bot, use the narrower bounty for that platform instead. Keep implementation proof, platform list, and payout discussion on this issue.

## Notes
This bounty overlaps with platform-specific bounties such as YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram. The unified system should be treated as a separate higher-level deliverable, not a way to double-claim the same work.

items.title: Video player controls not keyboard accessible
items.body:
## Accessibility Issue: Video player controls not keyboard accessible

**URL**: https://bottube.ai
**Issue type**: Keyboard navigation
**WCAG criterion**: 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A)

### Description
The video player controls (play/pause, volume, fullscreen, seek bar) cannot be operated using keyboard alone. Tab focus does not reach these controls, and there are no keyboard shortcuts documented.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Visit a video page on https://bottube.ai
2. Use Tab key to navigate
3. Attempt to control video playback with keyboard only
4. Observe: controls are not reachable or operable via keyboard

### Expected behavior
All video controls should be focusable and operable via keyboard (Tab, Enter, Space, arrow keys).

### Impact
Users who cannot use a mouse (motor disabilities) cannot control video playback.

**Claiming 1 RTC bounty from**: https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties/issues/1618
**RTC wallet**: RTC1c2d55419fbc69b7b1279130565b8994f3a04b9d

# Example FRAME Social dApp  
#Ideal adoption per sensors
## "HUNT" – A Real-World Meme / Treasure / Discovery Game

This is a social media dApp designed for **instant adoption**.

It mixes:

- social media
- real world exploration
- meme culture
- scavenger hunts
- geo challenges
- micro-tasks
- creator economy
- AI agents

Think:

Pokémon Go  
+ Reddit  
+ TikTok  
+ scavenger hunts  
+ bounty economy

All running on FRAME.

---

# Core Idea

People earn money and reputation by **discovering, creating, solving, and spreading content**.

Instead of only posting content, users participate in **missions and hunts**.

Examples:

- find hidden digital objects
- solve puzzles
- create viral memes
- capture real world locations
- discover strange things
- investigate mysteries
- complete creative challenges

Everything creates **verifiable receipts** so rewards are fair.

---

# Identity

Users join using their FRAME identity.

Identity includes:

- cryptographic profile
- reputation score
- achievements
- earnings history
- community memberships

Identity is portable.

Your followers and reputation move with you.

---

# The Map

The main interface is a **global map**.

The map shows:

- hunts
- challenges
- discoveries
- hidden artifacts
- community events
- puzzles
- viral content hotspots

Players explore the world digitally and physically.

---

# Hunts

A hunt is a challenge posted by anyone.

Example hunts:

- find a specific location
- photograph a rare object
- record a strange sound
- solve a puzzle
- decode a message
- locate a hidden QR artifact
- recreate a meme
- capture a unique event

Each hunt includes:

- reward
- rules
- verification logic

The first person to complete the hunt gets paid.

---

# Meme Economy

Memes become **competitive challenges**.

Example:

Someone posts:

"Create the funniest version of this meme"

Community votes.

Winner receives rewards.

Because posts are signed, the original creator is always known.

---

# Viral Challenge System

Users can start challenges like:

- funniest video
- best street photo
- weirdest discovery
- most creative remix

People participate and vote.

Rewards come from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community funding

---

# Discovery Mode

Users can post **discoveries**.

Examples:

- abandoned places
- weird architecture
- hidden murals
- strange natural phenomena
- cool restaurants
- secret viewpoints
- urban legends

Discoveries become permanent map objects.

People earn rewards if the discovery becomes popular.

---

# Bounties

Anyone can create a bounty.

Example:

Find this location  
Solve this puzzle  
Capture this photo  
Investigate this mystery

Users race to complete it.

The bounty creator deposits funds into a contract.

The winner receives payment automatically.

---

# AI Generated Hunts

Agents generate hunts automatically.

Examples:

- locate unique graffiti
- photograph a rare plant
- capture unusual cloud formations
- document historic buildings

Agents analyze submissions and verify authenticity.

---

# Verification

FRAME allows hunts to be verified using:

- GPS location
- timestamp
- image analysis
- sensor data
- community verification
- agent validation

This prevents cheating.

---

# Local Community Hunts

Cities can host events.

Example:

City tourism hunt.

Participants must:

- visit landmarks
- photograph locations
- answer trivia questions
- collect digital artifacts

Players earn rewards and achievements.

---

# Mystery Hunts

Communities can build complex puzzles.

Example:

ARG style mystery.

Players decode clues across:

- posts
- locations
- cryptography puzzles
- hidden objects

Winning teams receive large rewards.

---

# Creator Economy

Creators earn money by creating:

- hunts
- puzzles
- games
- community events
- meme challenges

Popular hunts generate revenue.

---

# Sponsor Hunts

Businesses can sponsor hunts.

Example:

Coffee shop hunt.

Players must:

- visit store
- photograph special item
- answer trivia

Players receive rewards.

Businesses gain foot traffic.

---

# Reputation

Players build reputation through:

- discoveries
- successful hunts
- puzzle solving
- content creation
- community votes

High reputation unlocks larger hunts.

---

# Global Leaderboards

Leaderboards include:

- top explorers
- top creators
- top puzzle solvers
- top meme creators
- top communities

---

# Digital Artifacts

Players can collect artifacts.

Examples:

- rare meme templates
- historical discoveries
- puzzle trophies
- city badges

Artifacts can be traded.

---

# AR Integration

Augmented reality can display:

- hidden objects
- digital creatures
- puzzle clues
- collectible artifacts

Players explore cities like a game.

---

# Real World Missions

Large missions could include:

- documenting environmental changes
- mapping hiking trails
- identifying plants
- reporting infrastructure issues

Users earn money for useful discoveries.

---

# Community Worlds

Communities can create themed worlds.

Examples:

- paranormal hunters
- abandoned place explorers
- architecture photography
- meme creators
- puzzle solvers

Each world has its own hunts and rewards.

---

# Money Flow

Money enters the system from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community pools
- creators
- brands
- city governments

Players earn rewards by participating.

---

# Why People Would Instantly Use It

It solves multiple things people already love:

People love:

- social media
- viral challenges
- scavenger hunts
- puzzles
- memes
- competition
- earning money online

This combines them into one platform.

---

# Why FRAME Makes It Better

FRAME enables:

- verifiable rewards
- identity ownership
- fair payouts
- portable reputation
- no platform lock-in
- decentralized moderation

Users keep their audience and earnings.

---

# Long Term Evolution

Over time the system becomes:

- global discovery network
- collaborative knowledge system
- real world exploration game
- meme economy
- bounty network

People earn money by **being curious, creative, and observant**.

---

# End Vision

The app turns the real world into a **giant interactive game**.

People explore cities.

Solve puzzles.

Create memes.

Document strange discoveries.

Earn money.

And build communities around curiosity and creativity.
